University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県天理市櫟本町字宮山2490', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県天理市櫟本町櫟本', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} [[JA:和爾下神社 (天理市)]] {{wikidata link|Q59282589}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wanishita Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=230}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 |祭神 = {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = Shikinaisha (small)
former village shrine
shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha |創建 = |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575}}
cypress bark roof |例祭 = July 14 (Gion Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}}
October 14 (Autumn Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 12.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 6.58 |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Kakinomoto Miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan).jpg|thumb|150px|(Reference image) Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan collection)]] '''Wanishita Shrine''' (わにしたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ichinomoto-cho|Q17174137}}, {{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932}}, formerly a {{jalink|village shrine|Q712534}}, and a {{jalink|shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|Q712534}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=72}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined in the center{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. === History === The shrine stands where the old Tatsuta Road and {{jalink|Kami-kaido (Kami-tsu-michi)|Q11360095|Kami-tsu-michi}} intersect{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In contrast to {{jalink|Wanishita Shrine (Yamatokoriyama)|Q59282593|Wanishita Shrine}} (Shimoharumichi-no-miya), about 2.5 {{jalink|kilometers|Q828224}} west in {{jalink|Yamatokoriyama|Q118213}}, it is also called Kamiharumichi-no-miya or Kamiharumichi Tenno{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. It is considered one of the "two seats of Wanishita Shrine" listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}} for Soekami District{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Originally, this shrine appears to have enshrined the ancestral deity of the Kakinomoto clan, relatives of the {{jalink|Wani clan|Q11417873}}, and that original deity may have been the Wani ancestor {{jalink|Ama-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. The shrine site was in Ichii-no-sho, an estate of {{jalink|Todai-ji|Q460367}}. ''{{jalink|Todai-ji Zoku Yoroku|Q11526471}}'' records that from the Jingo-keiun era, irrigation ponds and waterways were developed and Yokota Road (Tatsuta Road) was repaired{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. ''{{jalink|Kakinomoto Ason Hitomaro Kanmon}}'' says, "Beside Isonokami Temple in Soekami District there is a shrine called Harumichi-sha, and within it a temple called Kakinomoto-dera," indicating the old use of the name Harumichi and the existence of Kakinomoto-dera as the shrine-temple{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Nara-period roof tiles were excavated from Hitomaru-zuka in the former precincts of Kakinomoto-dera{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, it was called Kakinomoto-no-miya, and the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} ''{{jalink|Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala}}'' (in the collection of {{jalink|Yamato Bunkakan|Q3329639}}, a national {{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}) depicts this shrine{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. After the {{jalink|Nanboku-cho period|Q264012|Northern and Southern Courts period}}, it was repeatedly used as a military encampment{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Precincts === At the western foot of {{jalink|Mount Todai-ji}}, the precinct corresponds to the rear circular section of {{jalink|Wanishita Shrine Kofun}}, part of the {{jalink|Todai-ji-yama Kofun group}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Peripheral shrines === * Wakamiya - {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} * Juni Shrine - {{jalink|Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|Kumano Okami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Itsukushima Shrine - {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Kotohira Shrine - {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Otoshi Shrine - {{jalink|Toshigami#Otoshi-gami|Q3028663|Otoshi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Ebisu Shrine - {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Amateru Kotai Jingu - {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amateru Kotai Omikami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Inari Shrine - {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Tenmangu - {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Konpira-gu - Onamuchi-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Inari Shrine - {{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Sarutahiko Shrine - {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Main hall A three-bay {{jalink|kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575}} structure with cypress bark roof, built in the {{jalink|Azuchi-Momoyama period|Q319531|Momoyama period}}. National Important Cultural Property.[http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine (Tenri City site)] === Site information === ;Location *{{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 ;Access * Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Sakurai Line|Q865801}}{{jalink|Ichinomoto Station|Q3936059}}, about 5 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{jalink|Meicho Shuppan}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefectural History}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines of Yamato and Kii|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Tenri City History Vol. 1|editor=Tenri City History Compilation Committee|publisher=Tenri Jiho-sha|year=1976|ref={{sfnref|Tenri City History}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928861449}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine, Kakinomoto-dera ruins, and Uta-zuka] - Tenri City {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:祇園神社]][[Category:qq]]

'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} [[JA:和爾下神社 (天理市)]] {{wikidata link|Q59282589}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wanishita Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=230}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 |祭神 = {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = Shikinaisha (small)
former village shrine
shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha |創建 = |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575}}
cypress bark roof |例祭 = July 14 (Gion Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}}
October 14 (Autumn Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 12.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 6.58 |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Kakinomoto Miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan).jpg|thumb|150px|(Reference image) Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan collection)]] '''Wanishita Shrine''' (わにしたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ichinomoto-cho|Q17174137}}, {{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932}}, formerly a {{jalink|village shrine|Q712534}}, and a {{jalink|shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|Q712534}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=72}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined in the center{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. === History === The shrine stands where the old Tatsuta Road and {{jalink|Kami-kaido (Kami-tsu-michi)|Q11360095|Kami-tsu-michi}} intersect{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In contrast to {{jalink|Wanishita Shrine (Yamatokoriyama)|Q59282593|Wanishita Shrine}} (Shimoharumichi-no-miya), about 2.5 {{jalink|kilometers|Q828224}} west in {{jalink|Yamatokoriyama|Q118213}}, it is also called Kamiharumichi-no-miya or Kamiharumichi Tenno{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. It is considered one of the "two seats of Wanishita Shrine" listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}} for Soekami District{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Originally, this shrine appears to have enshrined the ancestral deity of the Kakinomoto clan, relatives of the {{jalink|Wani clan|Q11417873}}, and that original deity may have been the Wani ancestor {{jalink|Ama-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. The shrine site was in Ichii-no-sho, an estate of {{jalink|Todai-ji|Q460367}}. ''{{jalink|Todai-ji Zoku Yoroku|Q11526471}}'' records that from the Jingo-keiun era, irrigation ponds and waterways were developed and Yokota Road (Tatsuta Road) was repaired{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. ''{{jalink|Kakinomoto Ason Hitomaro Kanmon}}'' says, "Beside Isonokami Temple in Soekami District there is a shrine called Harumichi-sha, and within it a temple called Kakinomoto-dera," indicating the old use of the name Harumichi and the existence of Kakinomoto-dera as the shrine-temple{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Nara-period roof tiles were excavated from Hitomaru-zuka in the former precincts of Kakinomoto-dera{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, it was called Kakinomoto-no-miya, and the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}} ''{{jalink|Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala}}'' (in the collection of {{jalink|Yamato Bunkakan|Q3329639}}, a national {{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}) depicts this shrine{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. After the {{jalink|Nanboku-cho period|Q264012|Northern and Southern Courts period}}, it was repeatedly used as a military encampment{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Precincts === At the western foot of {{jalink|Mount Todai-ji}}, the precinct corresponds to the rear circular section of {{jalink|Wanishita Shrine Kofun}}, part of the {{jalink|Todai-ji-yama Kofun group}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Peripheral shrines === * Wakamiya - {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} * Juni Shrine - {{jalink|Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|Kumano Okami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Itsukushima Shrine - {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Kotohira Shrine - {{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Otoshi Shrine - {{jalink|Toshigami#Otoshi-gami|Q3028663|Otoshi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Ebisu Shrine - {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Amateru Kotai Jingu - {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amateru Kotai Omikami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Inari Shrine - {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Tenmangu - {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Konpira-gu - Onamuchi-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Inari Shrine - {{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Sarutahiko Shrine - {{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Main hall A three-bay {{jalink|kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575}} structure with cypress bark roof, built in the {{jalink|Azuchi-Momoyama period|Q319531|Momoyama period}}. National Important Cultural Property.[http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine (Tenri City site)] === Site information === ;Location *{{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Tenri|Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 ;Access * Nearest station: JR {{jalink|Sakurai Line|Q865801}}{{jalink|Ichinomoto Station|Q3936059}}, about 5 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{jalink|Meicho Shuppan}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefectural History}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines of Yamato and Kii|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Tenri City History Vol. 1|editor=Tenri City History Compilation Committee|publisher=Tenri Jiho-sha|year=1976|ref={{sfnref|Tenri City History}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928861449}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine, Kakinomoto-dera ruins, and Uta-zuka] - Tenri City {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:天理市の建築物]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:祇園神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} [[JA:和爾下神社 (天理市)]] {{wikidata link|Q59282589}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wanishita Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Wanishita Shrine October 14 (Autumn Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 12.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 6.58 |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Kakinomoto Miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan).jpg|thumb|150px|(Reference image) Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan collection)]] '''Wanishita Shrine''' (わにしたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomoto-cho|Q17174137|WD=Q17174137}}, {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, formerly a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and a {{ill|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=72}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined in the center{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. === History === The shrine stands where the old Tatsuta Road and {{ill|lt=Kami-kaido (Kami-tsu-michi)|Q11360095|WD=Q11360095|lt=Kami-tsu-michi}} intersect{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Wanishita Shrine (Yamatokoriyama)|Q59282593|WD=Q59282593|lt=Wanishita Shrine}} (Shimoharumichi-no-miya), about 2.5 {{ill|lt=kilometers|Q828224|WD=Q828224}} west in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, it is also called Kamiharumichi-no-miya or Kamiharumichi Tenno{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. It is considered one of the "two seats of Wanishita Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for Soekami District{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Originally, this shrine appears to have enshrined the ancestral deity of the Kakinomoto clan, relatives of the {{ill|lt=Wani clan|Q11417873|WD=Q11417873}}, and that original deity may have been the Wani ancestor {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. The shrine site was in Ichii-no-sho, an estate of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367}}. ''{{ill|lt=Todai-ji Zoku Yoroku|Q11526471|WD=Q11526471}}'' records that from the Jingo-keiun era, irrigation ponds and waterways were developed and Yokota Road (Tatsuta Road) was repaired{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. ''{{ill||lt=Kakinomoto Ason Hitomaro Kanmon|UNKNOWN}}'' says, "Beside Isonokami Temple in Soekami District there is a shrine called Harumichi-sha, and within it a temple called Kakinomoto-dera," indicating the old use of the name Harumichi and the existence of Kakinomoto-dera as the shrine-temple{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Nara-period roof tiles were excavated from Hitomaru-zuka in the former precincts of Kakinomoto-dera{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, it was called Kakinomoto-no-miya, and the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} ''{{ill||lt=Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala|UNKNOWN}}'' (in the collection of {{ill|lt=Yamato Bunkakan|Q3329639|WD=Q3329639}}, a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) depicts this shrine{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. After the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-cho period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Northern and Southern Courts period}}, it was repeatedly used as a military encampment{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Precincts === At the western foot of {{ill||lt=Mount Todai-ji|UNKNOWN}}, the precinct corresponds to the rear circular section of {{ill||lt=Wanishita Shrine Kofun|UNKNOWN}}, part of the {{ill||lt=Todai-ji-yama Kofun group|UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Peripheral shrines === * Wakamiya - {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} * Juni Shrine - {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Okami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Itsukushima Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Kotohira Shrine - {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Otoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Toshigami#Otoshi-gami|Q3028663|WD=Q3028663|lt=Otoshi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Ebisu Shrine - {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Amateru Kotai Jingu - {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Kotai Omikami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Inari Shrine - Onamuchi-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Sarutahiko Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Main hall A three-bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} structure with cypress bark roof, built in the {{ill|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531|lt=Momoyama period}}. National Important Cultural Property.[http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine (Tenri City site)] === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 ;Access * Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomoto Station|Q3936059|WD=Q3936059}}, about 5 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill||lt=Meicho Shuppan|UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefectural History}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines of Yamato and Kii|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Tenri City History Vol. 1|editor=Tenri City History Compilation Committee|publisher=Tenri Jiho-sha|year=1976|ref={{sfnref|Tenri City History}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928861449}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine, Kakinomoto-dera ruins, and Uta-zuka] - 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} [[JA:和爾下神社 (天理市)]] {{wikidata link|Q59282589}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wanishita Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=230}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |社格 = Shikinaisha (small)
former village shrine
shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha |創建 = |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}}
cypress bark roof |例祭 = July 14 (Gion Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}}
October 14 (Autumn Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 12.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 6.58 |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Kakinomoto Miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan).jpg|thumb|150px|(Reference image) Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan collection)]] '''Wanishita Shrine''' (わにしたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomoto-cho|Q17174137|WD=Q17174137}}, {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, formerly a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and a {{ill|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=72}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined in the center{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. === History === The shrine stands where the old Tatsuta Road and {{ill|lt=Kami-kaido (Kami-tsu-michi)|Q11360095|WD=Q11360095|lt=Kami-tsu-michi}} intersect{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Wanishita Shrine (Yamatokoriyama)|Q59282593|WD=Q59282593|lt=Wanishita Shrine}} (Shimoharumichi-no-miya), about 2.5 {{ill|lt=kilometers|Q828224|WD=Q828224}} west in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, it is also called Kamiharumichi-no-miya or Kamiharumichi Tenno{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. It is considered one of the "two seats of Wanishita Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for Soekami District{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Originally, this shrine appears to have enshrined the ancestral deity of the Kakinomoto clan, relatives of the {{ill|lt=Wani clan|Q11417873|WD=Q11417873}}, and that original deity may have been the Wani ancestor {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. The shrine site was in Ichii-no-sho, an estate of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367}}. ''{{ill|lt=Todai-ji Zoku Yoroku|Q11526471|WD=Q11526471}}'' records that from the Jingo-keiun era, irrigation ponds and waterways were developed and Yokota Road (Tatsuta Road) was repaired{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kakinomoto Ason Hitomaro Kanmon|UNKNOWN}}'' says, "Beside Isonokami Temple in Soekami District there is a shrine called Harumichi-sha, and within it a temple called Kakinomoto-dera," indicating the old use of the name Harumichi and the existence of Kakinomoto-dera as the shrine-temple{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Nara-period roof tiles were excavated from Hitomaru-zuka in the former precincts of Kakinomoto-dera{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, it was called Kakinomoto-no-miya, and the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala|UNKNOWN}}'' (in the collection of {{ill|lt=Yamato Bunkakan|Q3329639|WD=Q3329639}}, a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) depicts this shrine{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. After the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-cho period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Northern and Southern Courts period}}, it was repeatedly used as a military encampment{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Precincts === At the western foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mount Todai-ji|UNKNOWN}}, the precinct corresponds to the rear circular section of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wanishita Shrine Kofun|UNKNOWN}}, part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Todai-ji-yama Kofun group|UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Peripheral shrines === * Wakamiya - {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} * Juni Shrine - {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Okami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Itsukushima Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Kotohira Shrine - {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Otoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Toshigami#Otoshi-gami|Q3028663|WD=Q3028663|lt=Otoshi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Ebisu Shrine - {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Amateru Kotai Jingu - {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Kotai Omikami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Tenmangu - {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Konpira-gu - Onamuchi-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Sarutahiko Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Main hall A three-bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} structure with cypress bark roof, built in the {{ill|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531|lt=Momoyama period}}. National Important Cultural Property.[http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine (Tenri City site)] === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 ;Access * Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomoto Station|Q3936059|WD=Q3936059}}, about 5 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Shuppan|UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefectural History}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines of Yamato and Kii|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Tenri City History Vol. 1|editor=Tenri City History Compilation Committee|publisher=Tenri Jiho-sha|year=1976|ref={{sfnref|Tenri City History}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928861449}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine, Kakinomoto-dera ruins, and Uta-zuka] - 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} [[JA:和爾下神社 (天理市)]] {{wikidata link|Q59282589}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wanishita Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=230}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = Shikinaisha (small)
former village shrine
shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha |創建 = |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}}
cypress bark roof |例祭 = July 14 (Gion Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}}
October 14 (Autumn Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 12.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 6.58 |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Kakinomoto Miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan).jpg|thumb|150px|(Reference image) Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan collection)]] '''Wanishita Shrine''' (わにしたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomoto-cho|Q17174137|WD=Q17174137}}, {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, formerly a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and a {{ill|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=72}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined in the center{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. === History === The shrine stands where the old Tatsuta Road and {{ill|lt=Kami-kaido (Kami-tsu-michi)|Q11360095|WD=Q11360095|lt=Kami-tsu-michi}} intersect{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Wanishita Shrine (Yamatokoriyama)|Q59282593|WD=Q59282593|lt=Wanishita Shrine}} (Shimoharumichi-no-miya), about 2.5 {{ill|lt=kilometers|Q828224|WD=Q828224}} west in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, it is also called Kamiharumichi-no-miya or Kamiharumichi Tenno{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. It is considered one of the "two seats of Wanishita Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for Soekami District{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Originally, this shrine appears to have enshrined the ancestral deity of the Kakinomoto clan, relatives of the {{ill|lt=Wani clan|Q11417873|WD=Q11417873}}, and that original deity may have been the Wani ancestor {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. The shrine site was in Ichii-no-sho, an estate of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367}}. ''{{ill|lt=Todai-ji Zoku Yoroku|Q11526471|WD=Q11526471}}'' records that from the Jingo-keiun era, irrigation ponds and waterways were developed and Yokota Road (Tatsuta Road) was repaired{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kakinomoto Ason Hitomaro Kanmon|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says, "Beside Isonokami Temple in Soekami District there is a shrine called Harumichi-sha, and within it a temple called Kakinomoto-dera," indicating the old use of the name Harumichi and the existence of Kakinomoto-dera as the shrine-temple{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Nara-period roof tiles were excavated from Hitomaru-zuka in the former precincts of Kakinomoto-dera{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, it was called Kakinomoto-no-miya, and the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (in the collection of {{ill|lt=Yamato Bunkakan|Q3329639|WD=Q3329639}}, a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) depicts this shrine{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. After the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-cho period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Northern and Southern Courts period}}, it was repeatedly used as a military encampment{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Precincts === At the western foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mount Todai-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}, the precinct corresponds to the rear circular section of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wanishita Shrine Kofun|1=UNKNOWN}}, part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Todai-ji-yama Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Peripheral shrines === * Wakamiya - {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} * Juni Shrine - {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Okami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Itsukushima Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Kotohira Shrine - {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Otoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Toshigami#Otoshi-gami|Q3028663|WD=Q3028663|lt=Otoshi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Ebisu Shrine - {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Amateru Kotai Jingu - {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Kotai Omikami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Tenmangu - {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Konpira-gu - Onamuchi-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Sarutahiko Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Main hall A three-bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} structure with cypress bark roof, built in the {{ill|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531|lt=Momoyama period}}. National Important Cultural Property.[http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine (Tenri City site)] === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 ;Access * Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomoto Station|Q3936059|WD=Q3936059}}, about 5 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Shuppan|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefectural History}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines of Yamato and Kii|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Tenri City History Vol. 1|editor=Tenri City History Compilation Committee|publisher=Tenri Jiho-sha|year=1976|ref={{sfnref|Tenri City History}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928861449}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine, Kakinomoto-dera ruins, and Uta-zuka] - (Ichinomoto) Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=230}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = Shikinaisha (small)
former village shrine
shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha |創建 = |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}}
cypress bark roof |例祭 = July 14 (Gion Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}}
October 14 (Autumn Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 12.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 6.58 |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Kakinomoto Miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan).jpg|thumb|150px|(Reference image) Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan collection)]] '''Wanishita Shrine''' (わにしたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomoto-cho|Q17174137|WD=Q17174137}}, {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, formerly a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and a {{ill|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=72}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined in the center{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. === History === The shrine stands where the old Tatsuta Road and {{ill|lt=Kami-kaido (Kami-tsu-michi)|Q11360095|WD=Q11360095|lt=Kami-tsu-michi}} intersect{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Wanishita Shrine (Yamatokoriyama)|Q59282593|WD=Q59282593|lt=Wanishita Shrine}} (Shimoharumichi-no-miya), about 2.5 {{ill|lt=kilometers|Q828224|WD=Q828224}} west in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, it is also called Kamiharumichi-no-miya or Kamiharumichi Tenno{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. It is considered one of the "two seats of Wanishita Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for Soekami District{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Originally, this shrine appears to have enshrined the ancestral deity of the Kakinomoto clan, relatives of the {{ill|lt=Wani clan|Q11417873|WD=Q11417873}}, and that original deity may have been the Wani ancestor {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. The shrine site was in Ichii-no-sho, an estate of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367}}. ''{{ill|lt=Todai-ji Zoku Yoroku|Q11526471|WD=Q11526471}}'' records that from the Jingo-keiun era, irrigation ponds and waterways were developed and Yokota Road (Tatsuta Road) was repaired{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kakinomoto Ason Hitomaro Kanmon|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says, "Beside Isonokami Temple in Soekami District there is a shrine called Harumichi-sha, and within it a temple called Kakinomoto-dera," indicating the old use of the name Harumichi and the existence of Kakinomoto-dera as the shrine-temple{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Nara-period roof tiles were excavated from Hitomaru-zuka in the former precincts of Kakinomoto-dera{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, it was called Kakinomoto-no-miya, and the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (in the collection of {{ill|lt=Yamato Bunkakan|Q3329639|WD=Q3329639}}, a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) depicts this shrine{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. After the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-cho period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Northern and Southern Courts period}}, it was repeatedly used as a military encampment{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Precincts === At the western foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mount Todai-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}, the precinct corresponds to the rear circular section of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wanishita Shrine Kofun|1=UNKNOWN}}, part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Todai-ji-yama Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Peripheral shrines === * Wakamiya - {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} * Juni Shrine - {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Okami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Itsukushima Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Kotohira Shrine - {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Otoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Toshigami#Otoshi-gami|Q3028663|WD=Q3028663|lt=Otoshi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Ebisu Shrine - {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Amateru Kotai Jingu - {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Kotai Omikami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Tenmangu - {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Konpira-gu - Onamuchi-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Sarutahiko Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Main hall A three-bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} structure with cypress bark roof, built in the {{ill|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531|lt=Momoyama period}}. National Important Cultural Property.[http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine (Tenri City site)] === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 ;Access * Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomoto Station|Q3936059|WD=Q3936059}}, about 5 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Shuppan|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefectural History}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines of Yamato and Kii|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Tenri City History Vol. 1|editor=Tenri City History Compilation Committee|publisher=Tenri Jiho-sha|year=1976|ref={{sfnref|Tenri City History}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928861449}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine, Kakinomoto-dera ruins, and Uta-zuka] - 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} [[JA:和爾下神社 (天理市)]] {{wikidata link|Q59282589}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wanishita Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=230}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = Shikinaisha (small)
former village shrine
shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha |創建 = |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}}
cypress bark roof |例祭 = July 14 (Gion Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}}
October 14 (Autumn Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 12.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 6.58 |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Kakinomoto Miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan).jpg|thumb|150px|(Reference image) Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan collection)]] '''Wanishita Shrine''' (わにしたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomoto-cho|Q17174137|WD=Q17174137}}, {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, formerly a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and a {{ill|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=72}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined in the center{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. === History === The shrine stands where the old Tatsuta Road and {{ill|lt=Kami-kaido (Kami-tsu-michi)|Q11360095|WD=Q11360095|lt=Kami-tsu-michi}} intersect{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Wanishita Shrine (Yamatokoriyama)|Q59282593|WD=Q59282593|lt=Wanishita Shrine}} (Shimoharumichi-no-miya), about 2.5 {{ill|lt=kilometers|Q828224|WD=Q828224}} west in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, it is also called Kamiharumichi-no-miya or Kamiharumichi Tenno{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. It is considered one of the "two seats of Wanishita Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for Soekami District{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Originally, this shrine appears to have enshrined the ancestral deity of the Kakinomoto clan, relatives of the {{ill|lt=Wani clan|Q11417873|WD=Q11417873}}, and that original deity may have been the Wani ancestor {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. The shrine site was in Ichii-no-sho, an estate of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367}}. ''{{ill|lt=Todai-ji Zoku Yoroku|Q11526471|WD=Q11526471}}'' records that from the Jingo-keiun era, irrigation ponds and waterways were developed and Yokota Road (Tatsuta Road) was repaired{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kakinomoto Ason Hitomaro Kanmon|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says, "Beside Isonokami Temple in Soekami District there is a shrine called Harumichi-sha, and within it a temple called Kakinomoto-dera," indicating the old use of the name Harumichi and the existence of Kakinomoto-dera as the shrine-temple{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Nara-period roof tiles were excavated from Hitomaru-zuka in the former precincts of Kakinomoto-dera{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, it was called Kakinomoto-no-miya, and the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (in the collection of {{ill|lt=Yamato Bunkakan|Q3329639|WD=Q3329639}}, a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) depicts this shrine{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. After the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-cho period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Northern and Southern Courts period}}, it was repeatedly used as a military encampment{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Precincts === At the western foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mount Todai-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}, the precinct corresponds to the rear circular section of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wanishita Shrine Kofun|1=UNKNOWN}}, part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Todai-ji-yama Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Peripheral shrines === * Wakamiya - {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} * Juni Shrine - {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Okami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Itsukushima Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Kotohira Shrine - {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Otoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Toshigami#Otoshi-gami|Q3028663|WD=Q3028663|lt=Otoshi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Ebisu Shrine - {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Amateru Kotai Jingu - {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Kotai Omikami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Tenmangu - {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Konpira-gu - Onamuchi-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Sarutahiko Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Main hall A three-bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} structure with cypress bark roof, built in the {{ill|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531|lt=Momoyama period}}. National Important Cultural Property.[http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine (Tenri City site)] === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 ;Access * Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomoto Station|Q3936059|WD=Q3936059}}, about 5 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Shuppan|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefectural History}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines of Yamato and Kii|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Tenri City History Vol. 1|editor=Tenri City History Compilation Committee|publisher=Tenri Jiho-sha|year=1976|ref={{sfnref|Tenri City History}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928861449}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine, Kakinomoto-dera ruins, and Uta-zuka] - 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} [[JA:和爾下神社 (天理市)]] {{wikidata link|Q59282589}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wanishita Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=230}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = Shikinaisha (small)
former village shrine
shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha |創建 = |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}}
cypress bark roof |例祭 = July 14 (Gion Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}}
October 14 (Autumn Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 12.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 6.58 |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Kakinomoto Miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan).jpg|thumb|150px|(Reference image) Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan collection)]] '''Wanishita Shrine''' (わにしたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomoto-cho|Q17174137|WD=Q17174137}}, {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, formerly a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and a {{ill|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=72}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined in the center{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. === History === The shrine stands where the old Tatsuta Road and {{ill|lt=Kami-kaido (Kami-tsu-michi)|Q11360095|WD=Q11360095|lt=Kami-tsu-michi}} intersect{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Wanishita Shrine (Yamatokoriyama)|Q59282593|WD=Q59282593|lt=Wanishita Shrine}} (Shimoharumichi-no-miya), about 2.5 {{ill|lt=kilometers|Q828224|WD=Q828224}} west in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, it is also called Kamiharumichi-no-miya or Kamiharumichi Tenno{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. It is considered one of the "two seats of Wanishita Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for Soekami District{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Originally, this shrine appears to have enshrined the ancestral deity of the Kakinomoto clan, relatives of the {{ill|lt=Wani clan|Q11417873|WD=Q11417873}}, and that original deity may have been the Wani ancestor {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. The shrine site was in Ichii-no-sho, an estate of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367}}. ''{{ill|lt=Todai-ji Zoku Yoroku|Q11526471|WD=Q11526471}}'' records that from the Jingo-keiun era, irrigation ponds and waterways were developed and Yokota Road (Tatsuta Road) was repaired{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kakinomoto Ason Hitomaro Kanmon|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says, "Beside Isonokami Temple in Soekami District there is a shrine called Harumichi-sha, and within it a temple called Kakinomoto-dera," indicating the old use of the name Harumichi and the existence of Kakinomoto-dera as the shrine-temple{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Nara-period roof tiles were excavated from Hitomaru-zuka in the former precincts of Kakinomoto-dera{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, it was called Kakinomoto-no-miya, and the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (in the collection of {{ill|lt=Yamato Bunkakan|Q3329639|WD=Q3329639}}, a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) depicts this shrine{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. After the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-cho period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Northern and Southern Courts period}}, it was repeatedly used as a military encampment{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Precincts === At the western foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mount Todai-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}, the precinct corresponds to the rear circular section of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wanishita Shrine Kofun|1=UNKNOWN}}, part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Todai-ji-yama Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Peripheral shrines === * Wakamiya - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} * Juni Shrine - {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Okami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Itsukushima Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Kotohira Shrine - {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Otoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Toshigami#Otoshi-gami|Q3028663|WD=Q3028663|lt=Otoshi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Ebisu Shrine - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Amateru Kotai Jingu - {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Kotai Omikami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Tenmangu - {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Konpira-gu - Onamuchi-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Sarutahiko Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Main hall A three-bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} structure with cypress bark roof, built in the {{ill|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531|lt=Momoyama period}}. National Important Cultural Property.[http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine (Tenri City site)] === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 ;Access * Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomoto Station|Q3936059|WD=Q3936059}}, about 5 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Shuppan|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefectural History}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines of Yamato and Kii|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Tenri City History Vol. 1|editor=Tenri City History Compilation Committee|publisher=Tenri Jiho-sha|year=1976|ref={{sfnref|Tenri City History}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928861449}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine, Kakinomoto-dera ruins, and Uta-zuka] - 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} [[JA:和爾下神社 (天理市)]] {{wikidata link|Q59282589}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wanishita Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=230}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = Shikinaisha (small)
former village shrine
shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha |創建 = |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}}
cypress bark roof |例祭 = July 14 (Gion Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}}
October 14 (Autumn Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 12.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 6.58 |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Kakinomoto Miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan).jpg|thumb|150px|(Reference image) Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan collection)]] '''Wanishita Shrine''' (わにしたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomoto-cho|Q17174137|WD=Q17174137}}, {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, formerly a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and a {{ill|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=72}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined in the center{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. === History === The shrine stands where the old Tatsuta Road and {{ill|lt=Kami-kaido (Kami-tsu-michi)|Q11360095|WD=Q11360095|lt=Kami-tsu-michi}} intersect{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Wanishita Shrine (Yamatokoriyama)|Q59282593|WD=Q59282593|lt=Wanishita Shrine}} (Shimoharumichi-no-miya), about 2.5 {{ill|lt=kilometers|Q828224|WD=Q828224}} west in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, it is also called Kamiharumichi-no-miya or Kamiharumichi Tenno{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. It is considered one of the "two seats of Wanishita Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for Soekami District{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Originally, this shrine appears to have enshrined the ancestral deity of the Kakinomoto clan, relatives of the {{ill|lt=Wani clan|Q11417873|WD=Q11417873}}, and that original deity may have been the Wani ancestor {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. The shrine site was in Ichii-no-sho, an estate of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367}}. ''{{ill|lt=Todai-ji Zoku Yoroku|Q11526471|WD=Q11526471}}'' records that from the Jingo-keiun era, irrigation ponds and waterways were developed and Yokota Road (Tatsuta Road) was repaired{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kakinomoto Ason Hitomaro Kanmon|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says, "Beside Isonokami Temple in Soekami District there is a shrine called Harumichi-sha, and within it a temple called Kakinomoto-dera," indicating the old use of the name Harumichi and the existence of Kakinomoto-dera as the shrine-temple{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Nara-period roof tiles were excavated from Hitomaru-zuka in the former precincts of Kakinomoto-dera{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, it was called Kakinomoto-no-miya, and the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (in the collection of {{ill|lt=Yamato Bunkakan|Q3329639|WD=Q3329639}}, a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) depicts this shrine{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. After the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-cho period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Northern and Southern Courts period}}, it was repeatedly used as a military encampment{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Precincts === At the western foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mount Todai-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}, the precinct corresponds to the rear circular section of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wanishita Shrine Kofun|1=UNKNOWN}}, part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Todai-ji-yama Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Peripheral shrines === * Wakamiya - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} * Juni Shrine - {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Okami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Itsukushima Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Kotohira Shrine - {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Otoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Toshigami#Otoshi-gami|Q3028663|WD=Q3028663|lt=Otoshi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Ebisu Shrine - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Amateru Kotai Jingu - {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Kotai Omikami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Tenmangu - {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Konpira-gu - Onamuchi-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Sarutahiko Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Main hall A three-bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} structure with cypress bark roof, built in the {{ill|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531|lt=Momoyama period}}. National Important Cultural Property.[http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine (Tenri City site)] === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}Ichinomoto-cho, Aza Miyayama 2490 ;Access * Nearest station: JR {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomoto Station|Q3936059|WD=Q3936059}}, about 5 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Shuppan|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefectural History}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines of Yamato and Kii|editor=Heibonsha|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|year=1997|isbn=4582134025|ref={{sfnref|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Tenri City History Vol. 1|editor=Tenri City History Compilation Committee|publisher=Tenri Jiho-sha|year=1976|ref={{sfnref|Tenri City History}}}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5928861449}} {{commonscat|Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|Wanishita Shrine}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/bunkazai/1391410354160.html Wanishita Shrine, Kakinomoto-dera ruins, and Uta-zuka] - {{Expand Japanese|和爾下神社 (天理市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) | native_name = 和爾下神社 | image = Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) | coordinates = {{coord|34.620093|135.835153}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)'''|和爾下神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)}} [[JA:和爾下神社 (天理市)]] {{wikidata link|Q59282589}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wanishita Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Wanishita Shrine October 14 (Autumn Festival) {{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 12.30 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 6.58 |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Kakinomoto Miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan).jpg|thumb|150px|(Reference image) Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala (Yamato Bunkakan collection)]] '''Wanishita Shrine''' (わにしたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ichinomoto-cho|Q17174137|WD=Q17174137}}, {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|WD=Q502196}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, formerly a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and a {{ill|lt=shinsen-heihaku-ryo kyoshinsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=72}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined in the center{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the left{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. * {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}} - enshrined on the right{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. === History === The shrine stands where the old Tatsuta Road and {{ill|lt=Kami-kaido (Kami-tsu-michi)|Q11360095|WD=Q11360095|lt=Kami-tsu-michi}} intersect{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In contrast to {{ill|lt=Wanishita Shrine (Yamatokoriyama)|Q59282593|WD=Q59282593|lt=Wanishita Shrine}} (Shimoharumichi-no-miya), about 2.5 {{ill|lt=kilometers|Q828224|WD=Q828224}} west in {{ill|lt=Yamatokoriyama|Q118213|WD=Q118213}}, it is also called Kamiharumichi-no-miya or Kamiharumichi Tenno{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. It is considered one of the "two seats of Wanishita Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for Soekami District{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Originally, this shrine appears to have enshrined the ancestral deity of the Kakinomoto clan, relatives of the {{ill|lt=Wani clan|Q11417873|WD=Q11417873}}, and that original deity may have been the Wani ancestor {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593}}{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}. The shrine site was in Ichii-no-sho, an estate of {{ill|lt=Todai-ji|Q460367|WD=Q460367}}. ''{{ill|lt=Todai-ji Zoku Yoroku|Q11526471|WD=Q11526471}}'' records that from the Jingo-keiun era, irrigation ponds and waterways were developed and Yokota Road (Tatsuta Road) was repaired{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kakinomoto Ason Hitomaro Kanmon|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says, "Beside Isonokami Temple in Soekami District there is a shrine called Harumichi-sha, and within it a temple called Kakinomoto-dera," indicating the old use of the name Harumichi and the existence of Kakinomoto-dera as the shrine-temple{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. Nara-period roof tiles were excavated from Hitomaru-zuka in the former precincts of Kakinomoto-dera{{sfn|Nara Prefectural History|p=329}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, it was called Kakinomoto-no-miya, and the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}} ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kakinomoto-no-miya Mandala|1=UNKNOWN}}'' (in the collection of {{ill|lt=Yamato Bunkakan|Q3329639|WD=Q3329639}}, a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) depicts this shrine{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. After the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-cho period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Northern and Southern Courts period}}, it was repeatedly used as a military encampment{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Precincts === At the western foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mount Todai-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}, the precinct corresponds to the rear circular section of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wanishita Shrine Kofun|1=UNKNOWN}}, part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Todai-ji-yama Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|Great Dictionary of Temples and Shrines|p=832}}. === Peripheral shrines === * Wakamiya - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=718}} * Juni Shrine - {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Okami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Itsukushima Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Kotohira Shrine - {{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Otoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Toshigami#Otoshi-gami|Q3028663|WD=Q3028663|lt=Otoshi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Ebisu Shrine - {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Sumiyoshi Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Amateru Kotai Jingu - {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Kotai Omikami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Inari Shrine - {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Tenmangu - {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=719}} * Konpira-gu - Onamuchi-no-Mikoto{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Inari Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} * Sarutahiko Shrine - {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}}{{sfn|Tenri City History|p=720}} === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Main hall A three-bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} structure with cypress bark roof, built in the {{ill|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531|lt=Momoyama period}}.
